doc_loadingoverlay_a11y_1: The component includes `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` for screen reader announcements.
doc_loadingoverlay_a11y_2: The `aria-busy="true"` attribute indicates that the content is loading.
doc_loadingoverlay_a11y_3: Ensure that loading messages are descriptive and helpful for users relying on assistive technologies.
doc_loadingoverlay_basic_desc: Mask overlay used to prevent interactions while content or pages are loading.
doc_loadingoverlay_bp_choose_appropriate: Use absolute positioning for partial page updates and fixed positioning for full-page operations.
doc_loadingoverlay_bp_consider_backdrop_: Use light backdrops on dark backgrounds and vice versa for better visibility.
doc_loadingoverlay_bp_provide_context_de: Include a message when the loading operation might take more than a few seconds.
doc_loadingoverlay_bp_timeout_handling_d: Always implement timeout handling to prevent indefinite loading states.
doc_loadingoverlay_bp_use_sparingly_desc: Only show loading overlays when necessary to avoid disrupting the user experience.
doc_loadingoverlay_desc: A component that overlays the entire screen or a specific element to display a loading state with customizable loaders, backdrops, and messages.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_backdrop: Light and dark backdrop options for different background colors.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_basic: A simple loading overlay with the default spinner.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_blur: Control the backdrop blur intensity: none, sm, md, or lg.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_custom: Provide custom content instead of the default loaders.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_full: Use fixed={true} to create a full-screen loading overlay.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_interactive: A practical example showing how to toggle the loading state.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_message: Display a message along with the loading indicator.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_types: Choose from various loader animations: spinner, bars, dots, or pulse.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_aria_desc: Provides necessary ARIA attributes for screen reader accessibility during loading.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_backdrop_desc: Light or dark backdrop with adjustable blur effects.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_custom_desc: Replace the default loader with custom content.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_message_desc: Display a loading message to provide context to users.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_position_desc: Can be positioned absolutely within a container or fixed for full-screen coverage.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_types_desc: Choose from spinner, bars, dots, or pulse animations.
doc_loadingoverlay_long_desc: Details and usage for the Loading Overlay component.
doc_loadingoverlay_title: Overlay to cover containers and indicate a loading state
doc_loadingoverlay_wtu_1: When you need to block user interaction during asynchronous operations like data fetching or form submission.
doc_loadingoverlay_wtu_2: When you want to provide visual feedback that a process is ongoing.
doc_loadingoverlay_wtu_3: When you need a full-screen loading indicator for application initialization.
doc_loadingoverlay_wtu_4: When you want to overlay a specific section of the page during partial updates.
doc_notification_best_practice_1: Use for information that is more important than a toast and needs to be clearly communicated, such as background task completions or important system updates.
doc_notification_best_practice_2: Provide a clear title and concise description to help users understand what happened and if any action is needed.
doc_notification_best_practice_3: Be mindful of screen real estate; avoid showing too many notifications at once as they might block important UI elements.
doc_notification_long_desc: Display a notification message globally in a corner of the system.
doc_notification_usage_desc: Used for system-level dynamic messages that are more complex or important than simple toasts.
doc_snackbar_adv_desc: When managing snackbars across the entire application, using a Provider is recommended.
doc_snackbar_long_desc: A notification component that temporarily displays processing results at the bottom of the screen.
doc_snackbar_title: A small message that appears briefly at the bottom of the screen to provide feedback.
doc_snackbar_usage_action_desc: Can include an action button that the user can interact with.
doc_snackbar_usage_close_desc: Displays a button that allows the user to manually close the snackbar.
doc_snackbar_usage_interactive_desc: You can see it in action.
doc_snackbar_var_desc: Use the `variant` property to apply styles based on state (Success, Error, etc.).
doc_toast_basic_usage_desc: Toasts combine a title, description, and variant (info, success, warning, error).
doc_toast_best_practice_1: Use to notify users of temporary, low-priority process results like 'Saved' or 'Deleted'.
doc_toast_best_practice_2: For critical errors requiring user action or confirmation, consider using Dialog or Alert.
doc_toast_best_practice_3: Keep messages short and concise, and limit the number of toasts displayed simultaneously on screen.
doc_toast_desc: Non-intrusive floating messages that provide feedback on system operations
doc_toast_interactive_example_desc: In the example below, you can interact with a toast to see how it works.
doc_toast_long_desc: The toast component is used to convey short notifications or feedback to the user. It usually appears in the corner of the screen and disappears automatically after a certain period of time.
doc_toast_provider_prop_children_desc: Children elements to be rendered within the provider
doc_toast_provider_prop_duration_desc: Duration for which the toast is displayed (in milliseconds)
doc_toast_provider_prop_position_desc: Toast display position (top-right, top-left, top-center, bottom-right, bottom-left, bottom-center)
doc_toast_system_usage: How to use the Toast System
doc_toast_system_usage_desc: To actually display a toast, it is recommended to wrap your application in a `ToastProvider` and use the `useToast` hook.
doc_toast_title: Non-intrusive floating messages that provide feedback on system operations
doc_toast_variants_desc: You can use the `variant` property to select a style appropriate for the type of notification.
doc_alert_basic_desc: The most common usage is to display both a title and a description.
doc_alert_close_button_desc: By specifying the `onClose` property, a button to dismiss the alert can be displayed.
doc_alert_error_desc: Indicates that a problem has occurred.
doc_alert_guideline_1: Use to communicate important information without interrupting the user's current task.
doc_alert_guideline_2: Choose the appropriate variant based on the nature of the message (Success, Info, Warning, Error).
doc_alert_guideline_3: For critical changes requiring immediate action or confirmation, consider using a Modal or Dialog.
doc_alert_long_desc: Alert components are used to convey important information, warnings, success messages, or errors to users.
doc_alert_no_title_desc: It is also possible to display only a description.
doc_alert_success_desc: Indicates that an action has been completed successfully.
doc_alert_variants_desc: Use the `variant` property to choose a style appropriate for the type of information.
doc_badge_best_practice_1: Keep badge text short and concise so the meaning is conveyed at a glance.
doc_badge_best_practice_2: Assign meaning to colors (semantic colors) and operate with consistent rules, like green for success and red for error.
doc_badge_guideline_1: Use when you want to subtly emphasize status (In Progress, Complete, etc.) or numerical counts (unread counts).
doc_badge_guideline_2: Can also be used as 'tags' to indicate category classification.
doc_badge_icon_only_desc: You can display a badge with only an icon by omitting the `content` property and specifying only the `icon`.
doc_badge_long_desc: The badge component is used to display status, notification counts, or short labels.
doc_badge_variants_desc: You can change the badge style using the `variant` property.
doc_badge_with_icon_desc: You can add an icon to the badge using the `icon` property.
doc_banner_best_practice_1: Use for high-priority, system-wide announcements that require immediate attention, such as maintenance alerts or critical service updates.
doc_banner_best_practice_2: Position banners at the top of the page for maximum visibility, but ensure they can be dismissed once the information is acknowledged.
doc_banner_best_practice_3: Avoid displaying multiple banners simultaneously to prevent visual clutter and information overload.
doc_banner_long_desc: A banner component to present important messages or actions to the user. Typically displayed at the top of the page.
doc_banner_title: Prominent banner for important messages and user actions
doc_emptystate_basic_desc: Combines an icon, title, description, and action buttons for display.
doc_emptystate_icons_actions_desc: It is also possible to combine only icons or multiple action buttons.
doc_emptystate_long_desc: A component used as a placeholder in the initial state when data is empty or there are no search results. It informs the user of the current situation and prompts them for the next action.
doc_emptystate_simple_desc: A simpler design that eliminates borders and background colors.
doc_emptystate_title: Component for guiding users when no data is available
